paragraphs: I'm a University of Washington student pursuing degrees in Informatics (Data Science) and Economics. I'm interested in software engineering, data engineering, and applied machine learning. I'm especially drawn to work that turns complex systems and messy data into useful products.

segments.text: Right now, I'm researching accessible sidewalk mapping at Howe Lab and building
, an AI badminton coach. I recently built research data pipelines at the UW eScience Institute, and I've led student engineering communities through

paragraphs: I'm currently looking for software, data engineering, and applied machine learning opportunities where I can ship useful systems and learn from a strong team.

education.degree: B.S. Informatics (Data Science) · B.A. Economics · GPA 3.66

resumeDownloadMessage: A concise overview of my experience, projects, and technical work.

experience.summary: Built an Airflow and Codex pipeline that identified 4,000+ economics studies and reduced dataset preparation from days to minutes.
Conducting reinforcement learning research to help map accessible sidewalks.
Built flight-delay models and data pipelines across 600,000+ records while developing optimization and sentiment-analysis tools.

leadership.summary: Mentored Cohort 5 builders through rapid product development and weekly milestones.

leadership.company: Software Engineering Career Club at UW
leadership.summary: Improving infrastructure for 2,000+ UW students while leading SWECC Labs and its open-source contributors.
Led the organization and its mission to connect UW students through shared meals.

projects.title: Mesocosm
projects.summary: Python CLI for scaffolding, submitting, and running reproducible sandbox environments for AI agents, with exportable replay data for showcases.

projects.title: IsoCourt
projects.summary: AI badminton coach that analyzes uploaded footage with action recognition and pose estimation to deliver stroke breakdowns and personalized technical feedback.
